PROFITEROLES
chapter 3 | page 52

Choux pastry ingredients:
- 1 cup water
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 4 eggs

Chocolate sauce:
- 8 oz semisweet chocolate [53], chopped
- 1/2 cup heavy cream
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon corn syrup
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract

Filling:
- 2 cups heavy cream
- 3 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Directions for choux paste:

Preheat oven 400°F. Line baking sheet with parchment.

Bring water, butter, salt to boil. Remove heat. Stir in flour
all at once, mixing until paste forms and pulls away from pan sides
[text smudged]. Cool 5 minutes.

Beat in eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition until
smooth and glossy. Batter should be stiff but piping consistency.

Transfer to piping bag fitted with 1/2 inch round tip. Pipe small
mounds 1 1/2 inches apart on prepared sheet. Bake 25-30 minutes
until puffed and golden brown. Do not open oven during cooking.

Cool on wire rack 15 minutes, then cool completely.

Chocolate sauce preparation:

Place chopped chocolate in heatproof bowl. Heat cream and butter
together until steaming. Pour over chocolate, let sit 1 minute.
Whisk until smooth and glossy. Stir in corn syrup and vanilla.
Keep warm for serving [54].

Assembly and serving:

Whip cold cream with powdered sugar and vanilla until stiff peaks
form. Slice cooled profiteroles in half horizontally. Pipe or
spoon cream into bottom half. Replace top.

Stack profiteroles in serving dish. Pour warm chocolate sauce over
top, allowing it to drizzle down sides. Serve immediately.

Alternative: Instead of fresh cream, use vanilla ice cream. Pour
chocolate sauce over warm profiteroles filled with ice cream.

Prep: 30 minutes
Baking: 30 minutes
Chill: 30 minutes
Total time: 90 minutes
Serves: 24-30 profiteroles

NOTE: Profiteroles can be made 1 day ahead. Store in airtight
container at room temperature. Fill with cream just before serving.
